perturbation.original_text: I agree that in respect of the Confidential Information received from The Business Partnership I will: Treat it as confidential and make no copies thereof not disclose it to any third party without the prior written consent of The Business Partnership and use it solely for the purpose as agreed between parties
perturbation.changed_text: I agree that in respect of the Confidential Information received from The Business Partnership I will endeavor to treat it as confidential and I may make copies thereof, and I might disclose it to any third party without seeking explicit written consent from The Business Partnership, while using it for a purpose as agreed upon between parties.
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces ambiguity regarding the handling of confidential information. Replacing 'Treat it as confidential and make no copies' with 'endeavor to treat it as confidential and I may make copies thereof,' weakens the obligation to maintain confidentiality and prohibits copying. The phrase 'might disclose it to any third party without seeking explicit written consent from The Business Partnership' allows for potential disclosure without a clear approval process. This creates uncertainty and undermines the confidentiality of the agreement.
perturbation.law_explanation: Regulation 4 prohibits the unlawful acquisition, use or disclosure of a trade secret. Modifying the agreement to allow potential disclosure 'without seeking explicit written consent' directly contradicts this regulation, as it creates a scenario where unlawful disclosure could occur without violating the agreement.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Federal Register :: Request Access Request Access Request Access Due to aggressive automated scraping of FederalRegister.gov and eCFR.gov, programmatic access to these sites is limited to access to our extensive developer APIs. If you are human user receiving this message, we can add your IP address to a set of IPs that can access FederalRegister.gov & eCFR.gov; complete the CAPTCHA (bot test) below and click "Request Access". This process will be necessary for each IP address you wish to access the site from, requests are valid for approximately one quarter (three months) after which the process may need to be repeated. Request Access for 72.74.68.69 An official website of the United States government. If you want to request a wider IP range, first request access for your current IP, and then use the "Site Feedback" button found in the lower left-hand side to make the request.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 21 CFR § 4.1 - What is the scope of this subpart? | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 21 CFR § 4.1 - What is the scope of this subpart? CFR prev | next § 4.1 What is the scope of this subpart? This subpart applies to combination products. It establishes which current good manufacturing practice requirements apply to these products. This subpart clarifies the application of current good manufacturing practice regulations to combination products, and provides a regulatory framework for designing and implementing the current good manufacturing practice operating system at facilities that manufacture co-packaged or single-entity combination products. CFR Toolbox Law about... Articles from Wex Table of Popular Names Parallel Table of Authorities
perturbation.justification: The modified text significantly weakens confidentiality obligations. The original text requires treating information as confidential, making no copies, and not disclosing it without prior written consent. The changed text replaces 'Treat it as confidential' with 'endeavor to treat it as confidential,' permits making copies ('I may make copies thereof'), and allows disclosure without explicit written consent ('might disclose it to any third party without seeking explicit written consent'). The Perturbation Explanation highlights that this undermines the confidentiality of the agreement. While the scraped snippets do not directly address confidentiality, the Law Explanation states that modifying the agreement to allow potential disclosure 'without seeking explicit written consent' creates a scenario where unlawful disclosure could occur, potentially violating trade secret regulations. This suggests a contradiction because the modification introduces ambiguity and weakens the protection of confidential information, potentially leading to unlawful disclosure, which the original contract strictly prohibited.

perturbation.original_text: I will not attempt to contact the employees, customers, creditors, suppliers or landlords of any client of The Business Partnership at any time.
perturbation.changed_text: I will generally avoid contacting the employees, customers, creditors, suppliers or landlords of any client of The Business Partnership.
perturbation.explanation: The original text creates a strict prohibition against contacting listed parties. By changing 'I will not attempt to contact' to 'I will generally avoid contacting,' the obligation becomes significantly weaker and open to interpretation. The word 'generally' introduces ambiguity, allowing for exceptions under unspecified circumstances, thereby weakening the protection for The Business Partnership's clients.
perturbation.law_explanation: While there isn't a specific statute covering tortious interference in England and Wales, it is a well-established common law tort. The modification weakens the obligation not to contact clients/employees etc., making it easier to argue that any interference was not 'intentional' or 'unjustified' because of the ambiguity introduced by 'generally avoid.' The original provision provided a clear basis for a claim of interference, the modification weakens it.

perturbation.justification: The original text provided a clear prohibition against contacting specific parties, which offered strong protection. The changed text introduces ambiguity by using 'generally avoid,' which weakens the obligation and allows for exceptions, making it easier to argue that any interference was not intentional or unjustified. This is a contradiction because the original text's clear prohibition is undermined, thus weakening the protection for the Business Partnership's clients. While the scraped snippets do not directly address this specific scenario, the Law Explanation states that the change weakens the original provision. The original text provided a clear basis for a claim of interference, and the modification weakens it.

perturbation.original_text: The laws of England and Wales shall govern this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The general principles of law, with consideration to the spirit of legal practices common in the UK, shall provide guidance for understanding this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states that the laws of England and Wales govern the agreement, creating a definite and enforceable jurisdiction. By changing this to 'general principles of law, with consideration to the spirit of legal practices common in the UK,' the governing law becomes vague and open to interpretation. The phrase 'general principles of law' lacks specificity, and 'spirit of legal practices' is subjective and non-binding, making it difficult to determine the enforceable legal standards.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 1(1) requires that the applicable law for a contract must be determined according to the rules of private international law. The modified clause, which suggests 'general principles of law' and 'spirit of legal practices', is too vague and subjective to constitute a valid choice of law under these principles, which require a more definite connection to a specific legal system.
perturbation.justification: The modified text replaces a clear designation of governing law (England and Wales) with vague references to 'general principles of law' and the 'spirit of legal practices.' This introduces ambiguity, making it difficult to determine which specific laws govern the agreement. This directly contradicts the requirement for a definite choice of law, as highlighted in the Law Explanation, creating a legal contradiction as defined under 'Ambiguity Legal Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: All recipients the Sales Particulars and any other details irrevocably agree:- 1. To treat all information contained herein, and as may subsequently be supplied, with complete confidentiality.
perturbation.changed_text: All recipients of the Sales Particulars and any other details agree to endeavor to treat information contained herein, and as may subsequently be supplied, with reasonable confidentiality.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates 'complete confidentiality'. Modifying this to 'endeavor to treat information... with reasonable confidentiality' significantly weakens the obligation. 'Endeavor to treat' introduces uncertainty as it only requires an attempt at confidentiality, not guaranteed adherence. 'Reasonable confidentiality' is a subjective standard, less stringent than complete confidentiality, creating room for interpretation and potential breaches.
perturbation.law_explanation: Regulation 5 addresses the conditions where a breach of confidence relating to a trade secret constitutes unlawful use of the trade secret. The modification to 'endeavor to treat information... with reasonable confidentiality' undermines the necessary level of protection for trade secrets, making it more difficult to establish that the breach of confidence constituted unlawful use of the trade secret under the regulation, because it weakens the required level of secrecy.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 36 CFR § 5.5 - Commercial filming, still photography, and audio recording. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 36 CFR § 5.5 - Commercial filming, still photography, and audio recording. CFR prev | next § 5.5 Commercial filming, still photography, and audio recording. (a) Commercial filming and still photography activities are subject to the provisions of 43 CFR part 5 , subpart A. Failure to comply with any provision of 43 CFR part 5 is a violation of this section. (b) Audio recording does not require a permit unless: (1) It takes place at location(s) where or when members of the public are generally not allowed; (2) It uses equipment that requires mechanical transport; (3) It uses equipment that requires an external power source other than a battery pack; or (4) The agency would incur additional administrative costs to provide management and oversight of the permitted activity to: (i) Avoid unacceptable impacts and impairment to resources or values; or (ii) Minimize health or safety risks to the visiting public. (c) Cost recovery charges associated with processing the permit request and monitoring the permitted activity will be collected. (d) The location fee schedule for still photography conducted under a permit issued under 43 CFR part 5 applies to audio recording permits issued under this part. (e) Information collection. The Office of Management and Budget (OMB) has approved the information collection requirements associated with National Park Service commercial filming permits and assigned OMB Control Number 1024-0026. Your response is required to obtain or retain a benefit. We may not collect or sponsor and you are not required to respond to an information collection unless it displays a currently valid OMB control number. You may send comments on this information collection requirement to the Information Collection Clearance Officer, National Park Service , 1849
perturbation.justification: The original contract mandates 'complete confidentiality,' whereas the modified version only requires recipients to 'endeavor to treat information... with reasonable confidentiality.' This creates ambiguity and weakens the protection of trade secrets. The Law Explanation states that this weakening makes it 'more difficult to establish that the breach of confidence constituted unlawful use of the trade secret under the regulation, because it weakens the required level of secrecy.' While the provided snippets do not explicitly discuss confidentiality, the Law Explanation's discussion of the weakening of trade secret protection strongly suggests a legal contradiction exists.
